trust.reasoning: Medium confidence - trust per claim ratio is 100%

content.summary: Rad AI (radai.com), founded February 2018 and headquartered in San Francisco, CA, is the leading generative AI company for radiology workflow automation and healthcare AI. The company builds and deploys proprietary Large Language Models (LLMs) trained on one of the world's largest radiology report datasets, powering three core products: Rad AI Reporting (AI-driven radiology reporting platform), Rad AI Impressions (automated impression generation, saving 60+ minutes per radiologist per shift), and Rad AI Continuity (AI-driven patient follow-up management). As of mid-2026, Rad AI's solutions are trusted by over 50% of all US health systems and 9 of the 10 largest US radiology practices, serving approximately 50 million patients annually. The company has raised $140M+ in total funding, most recently a $68M Series C (January–May 2025) led by Transformation Capital at a $525M valuation. Key investors include Khosla Ventures, ARTIS Ventures, WiL, Gradient Ventures (Google), and strategic health system partners Advocate Health, Memorial Hermann, Corewell Health, and Atlantic Health System. Rad AI's 9-person leadership team includes Co-founder/CEO Doktor Gurson and Co-founder/CPO Dr. Jeff Chang, joined in August 2025 by newly appointed CFO Barrett Dwyer and CRO Kevin Field to accelerate commercial growth. The company uses AWS (EC2 P4d/NVIDIA GPU instances) as its primary cloud infrastructure. With 3,229% three-year revenue growth, #107 on the 2025 Inc. 5000, #19 on the 2024 Deloitte Fast 500, and membership in the CNBC Disruptor 50, Rad AI is actively scaling and represents a strong enterprise healthcare AI buying signal — particularly for vendors selling into health system IT, clinical workflow, cloud infrastructure, or radiology/imaging spaces.
content.industry: Generative AI for Healthcare / Radiology AI / Health Technology
tech_stack: Amazon Web Services (AWS) — primary cloud provider
Amazon EC2 P4d instances (NVIDIA A100 GPU-powered) for model training and inference
Proprietary Large Language Models (LLMs) trained on radiology report datasets
Generative AI / NLP for medical report generation
EHR/RIS integration layer (AGFA HealthCare collaboration; HL7/DICOM-standard workflows)
content.headquarters: 548 Market St, PMB 49792, San Francisco, CA 94104
content.total_funding: $140M+ total raised; Series C totals $68M (initial $60M, January 2025, plus $8M strategic extension, May 2025); company valuation $525M
buying_signals: Recent major funding: $68M Series C closed Jan–May 2025; total $140M+ raised; valued at $525M — actively investing in platform scaling and expansion
Leadership build-out: CFO (Barrett Dwyer) and CRO (Kevin Field) hired August 2025, signaling commercial acceleration and pre-IPO/exit preparation
Three CMIOs appointed February 2025, deepening clinical credibility and enterprise sales capacity
Hyper-revenue growth: 3,229% three-year revenue growth; ranked #107 on 2025 Inc. 5000 and #7 on AI & Data sublist (August 2025)
Deloitte Technology Fast 500: #19 in 2024, #36 in 2025 — consistent top-tier growth trajectory
Record Q2 2025/2026: 45+ health systems and radiology practices signed or expanded partnerships in a single quarter
Market share expansion: from ~33% to 50%+ of US health systems adopting Rad AI solutions within ~12 months
Strategic investor-partnerships with Advocate Health, Memorial Hermann, Corewell Health, Atlantic Health System — driving enterprise expansion across their 100+ combined hospitals
CNBC Disruptor 50 recognition (June 2025) — category leadership signal
International market expansion: First international partnership (MIC Medical Imaging, Canada, July 2024); global scaling underway post-Series C
Active tradeshow presence: Exhibiting at AHRA 2026 (July 12–15, Orlando, FL) — actively pursuing new health system clients
Technology migration opportunity: AGFA HealthCare collaboration signals openness to technology ecosystem partnerships and third-party integrations
Actively hiring: 248 LinkedIn employees and growing team ('Radineers') — org expanding sales, engineering, and clinical teams
content.headcount_estimate: ~248 employees (LinkedIn, July 2026); self-described as '200+ Radineers'; LinkedIn company size band: 51–200
